stemHtml:
When calculating the Take-Off Distance for a Performance Class B multi-engine airplane, specific factors must be applied to the AFM performance data to take into account external variables that affect take-off performance. If these factors are not provided in the AFM or any other manuals from the manufacturer, which of the following regulation factors and conditions should be used?

1. Dry grass (on firm soil) requires a factor of 1.2 to be applied
2. If the condition of the grass (on firm soil), either dry or wet, cannot be determined, a factor of 1.15 is to be applied
3. Wet grass (on firm soil) may be slippery and therefore the RTO distance can increase significantly
4. Wet grass (on firm soil) requires a factor of 1.3 to be applied
5. A wet paved runway requires a factor of 1.3 to be applied
6. The length of grass defined in the applicable regulation is “up to 20 cm long”
